articles.title: Disney To Ax Nearly 1,000 Jobs Under New CEO Josh D'Amaro: Report
articles.summary: The Walt Disney Co. (NYSE: DIS) is reportedly preparing to lay off nearly 1,000 employees, primarily within its marketing department and through the merging of Disney+ and Hulu streaming services. These cost-cutting measures were initiated before new CEO Josh D'Amaro took leadership, who is focusing on fostering quicker collaboration across divisions. This restructuring follows previous layoffs in 2023 and 2025 and comes amidst other challenges like setbacks in tech investments and modest growth projections for its Experiences segment.

This restructuring follows previous layoffs in 2023 and 2025 and comes amidst other challenges like setbacks in tech investments and modest growth projections for its Experiences segment.

articles.title: Addenda Capital Inc. Sells 6,648 Shares of The Walt Disney Company $DIS
articles.summary: Addenda Capital Inc. has reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company by selling 6,648 shares, trimming its total holdings to 60,900 shares valued at approximately $6.97 million. Despite this reduction, many other institutional investors have increased their positions in Disney, with overall institutional ownership at about 65.71%. Analyst sentiment towards Disney remains largely positive, with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and a price target of $132.19, supported by several upgrades and maintained "Buy" ratings from major firms.

articles.title: The Walt Disney Company to cut up to 1,000 jobs as marketing overhaul begins
articles.summary: The Walt Disney Company is set to cut up to 1,000 jobs, primarily within its marketing division, as part of a restructuring effort dubbed "Project Imagine." Led by new Chief Marketing Officer Asad Ayaz, this initiative aims to consolidate marketing operations and reduce costs. The layoffs will affect less than 1% of Disney's 231,000-strong workforce and were reportedly planned before Josh D'Amaro became CEO in March.

articles.title: Layoffs Expected to Hit The Walt Disney Company Soon
articles.summary: The Walt Disney Company is reportedly preparing for significant layoffs, potentially impacting up to 1,000 positions, as new CEO Josh D'Amaro implements an "enterprise marketing" structure and other consolidations. These cuts follow previous sizable layoffs under former CEO Bob Iger, with the company aiming to operate as "One Disney." Approximately 80% of Disney's 231,000 employees are in the Experiences division.
articles.text_to_analyze: Layoffs Expected to Hit The Walt Disney Company Soon. Approximately 80% of Disney's 231,000 employees are in the Experiences division.

articles.title: Disney plans extensive round of layoffs in the coming weeks
articles.summary: Walt Disney Co. is preparing for an extensive round of layoffs in the coming weeks, potentially impacting up to 1,000 employees. These cuts are primarily expected from the consolidation of Disney's marketing department and reflect broader challenges in Hollywood, such as declining theatrical revenues and shrinking linear TV audiences, as well as the smaller profits from streaming services. The move follows similar workforce reductions across the industry, including recent cuts by Sony Pictures Entertainment and previous layoffs by Disney after Bob Iger's return.

articles.title: JAKKS Pacific Hits the Runway with ‘Devil Wears Prada’ Collector Dolls
articles.summary: JAKKS Pacific has launched its first-ever Disney ily 4EVER collector dolls inspired by a live-action film, featuring Miranda Priestly and Andy Sachs from 'The Devil Wears Prada'. These dolls celebrate the upcoming release of The Devil Wears Prada 2 and come with detailed accessories reflecting the characters' iconic styles. The collector dolls are available exclusively at Walmart for $49.97 each.

articles.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Features AstraZeneca, HSBC, Walt Disney, Oak Valley Bancorp, and BV Financial
articles.summary: Zacks.com's latest Analyst Blog highlights key research reports on AstraZeneca (AZN), HSBC Holdings plc (HSBC), The Walt Disney Co. (DIS), Oak Valley Bancorp (OVLY), and BV Financial, Inc. (BVFL). The blog provides detailed insights into the performance, strategic initiatives, and potential challenges for each company, emphasizing Zacks' unique microcap research. It also promotes Zacks' stock selection strategies, claiming significant outperformance against the S&P 500 and identifying a "Stock Most Likely to Double."

articles.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Features AstraZeneca, HSBC, Walt Disney, Oak Valley Bancorp, and BV Financial
articles.summary: Zacks Equity Research has released its latest Analyst Blog, highlighting five companies: AstraZeneca, HSBC, Walt Disney, Oak Valley Bancorp, and BV Financial. The report provides insights into each company's recent performance, strategic advantages, and challenges, noting that some of these stocks have outperformed their respective sectors. Zacks also offers information on its stock selection strategies and a special report on potential high-growth stocks.

articles.title: Walt Disney Co. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
articles.summary: Shares of Walt Disney Co. (DIS) rose 3.55% to $99.18 on Wednesday, outperforming the broader market which also saw gains. This increase ended a two-day losing streak for the stock. The S&P 500 Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average also experienced significant rises.
